feedback.text: Hello, I would like to make a request in favor of adding a protection against the practice of destroying the validity and functionality of a Video Game. I am sure that there have been made many requests such as this, but the practice itself is quite anti-consumer as the rightful ownership given by a purchase of this sort of digital media is directly threatened by this sort of practice. To be more specific, it is not just the act of not continuing to sell a Video Game, nor is it the act of not continuing to provide service for a Video Game. The problem is simple: Companies should not be able to take away the product you paid for without any notice, nor should they be able to continue to try and stop you from making it work on your own personal computer if they are not willing to continue their services. This problem isn't necessarily limited to Video Games, but it is a problem that uniquely affects Video Games in particular. I am not asking for some sort of regulation which demands that Sellers must provide a constant service to their Video Games. I am asking for a protection against the Sellers destroying the Video Games that I paid for, without my ability to ever access it again, may that be through the personal setup of a new Server to play on if the Game is online only, or through my ability to even boot up the Game to play on my own time. Please consider adding such a protection for the sake of Digital Fairness.
